entry.title: XBLNR Down payment request
entry.content: In Spain, it is necessary to report down payments with VAT. When making an Automatic Payment in SAP Public Cloud, the XBLNR field is not carried over from the down payment request. How could the field be carried out in the automatic payment execution?

entry.title: Re: Hi Team,I'm Currently working on Log On with a Custom...
entry.content:
Hi

I don't think this is possible with trial accounts. The option to establish trust was made available recently but i'm not sure if it works in trial. I've tested the scenario and I have the same issue. I've done this with a non-trial account and works as expected.

For the trial I even had to manually add the callback because it wasn't even in the list and giving an error. So as i said, it might not be possible in trial

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Create a custom MDF Object with multiple records
entry.content:
Hi Eugene, All
@Eugene_D_Fourie
Is there a way to allow maximum two records only in the child object? For example, currently the child accepts multiple entries but the client requires maximum two entries allowed per user in a custom mdf portlet.

entry.title: Re: Restrict only two records on Custom MDF Portlet
entry.content:
H Raviraj

Just wondering if you had a solution to this requirement? Could you please share because I'm also looking for one.

entry.title: Re: No dedicated error message found (SWF_FLEX_WEBGUI010) in SAP GUI for workflow scenario WS0200047
entry.content:
Turns out it was my primarily my own fault because I didn't catch all the exceptions after some refactoring. I suppose I should not be surprised...

But there was more to learn when I continued after catching the exception and raising one that was in the signature: Avoid raising exceptions in MMPUR_WFL_CONTEXT_ENHANCE it you can - at least when setting custom attributes or work item priority.

The journey continued. When an exception of type CX_RSM_AGT_DETN_BUSI_EXCEPTION was raised in the RSM_BADI_STATIC_RULE implementation IS_BUSINESS_EXCEPTION was set. However, it was still not perfect. The root cause was passed along via the PREVIOUS exception, but in ME53N the message displayed was now "An exception was raised". Passing along the text ID of the previous exception was not a good solution either, because only the message definition text was displayed - the parameter values were lost.

I have debugged for several hours, into the following classes/methods and probably some more, and unfortunately my conclusion is that it isn't possible to pass on a message with parameters. In one of these methods (can't recall exactly where it was now) the "business exception" is copied from the exception, but it is only the text ID that is copied - I could not find any attempt to retrieve parameter values.

CL_RSM_QUERY_ENGINE > IF_RSM_QUERY_ENGINE~EXECUTE_RULE_IMPLEMENTATION
CL_RSM_QUERY_ENGINE > IF_RSM_QUERY_ENGINE~INVOKE_QUERY_ENGINE
CL_RSM_DETERMINE_RESPONSIBLE > IF_RSM_DETERMINE_RESPONSIBLE~DETERMINE_RESPONSIBLE
CL_SWF_FLEX_UTL_RESPY_RULE > IF_SWF_FLEX_UTL_RESPY_RULE~GET_USERS_FOR_RESPY_RULE
CL_SWF_FLEX_RUN_RESPY_RULE > IF_SWF_FLEX_RUN_AGENT~EVALUATE
CL_SWF_FLEX_RUN_AGENT > IF_SWF_FLEX_RUN_AGENT~EVALUATE
CL_SWF_FLEX_RUN_AGENT > RAISE_NO_AGENT_EXCEPTION
CL_SWF_FLEX_RUN_WORKFLOW > IF_SWF_FLEX_ACTIVITY~RESOLVE_AGENTS
CL_SWF_FLEX_ENGINE_ACTIVITY > EXECUTE (LCL_ON_NO_AGENT_FOUND_CALLER)
CL_MM_PUR_PR_FLEX_WFL > IF_SWF_FLEX_IFS_RUN_APPL~ON_NO_AGENT_FOUND

In the end I created a new message stating simply that an approver can't be found, with a long text telling the administrator that there should be additional information in the workflow log to tell you the root cause. I can't say I feel satisfied, but sometimes defeat has to be accepted.

From an end user perspective this new message - compared to "An exception was raised" - is at least saying something about what the problem is and how to proceed.

entry.title: How to change target mapping parameters for a fiori app?
entry.content:
Hello,

We want to use the F1511A - Create Maintenance Request app for creating notification type M1 - Maintenance Request. But as mentioned in the target mapping of the app - Notification type = Y1 desn't allow to create such notification.


If we try to modify the target mapping parameter values using fiori launchpad designer - the system shows the following warning. And it will break the reference with the tile and in the technical catalog everything gets deleted.


Does anyone have an idea, how to proceed with the such customization?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Moving Average Price changing after procurement of fr...
entry.content: A separate valuation area (plant) for free goods might be an option, however side effects have to be considered, e.g. increased master data volume, MRP planning plans on plant (MRP area) level so it won't consolidate regular stock and free goods.

entry.title: Re: Blog Series Posting Documents to SharePoint
entry.content:
Hi Luiz - Sorry I haven't received any support on this topic but then we had eventually built a product that works seamlessly with SAP and Microsoft Sharepoint. If you are interested in knowing more please feel free to reach out to us using the contact form (you can quote this link or my name while reaching out)  - https://versafile.com/docuflow/

Regards

Tushar

entry.title: Re: SOA-Management - MDG - Data Replication - SRT_CORE122 - Authorization missing
entry.content: Hallo Patrick, do you have a solution here? Thank you very much Anika

entry.title: Re: Screen Personas Slipstream engine – Footer Buttons no...
entry.content:
Webgui and Slipstream menu and toolbars structure can be different. If you will prepare Flavor in Webgui and consume in SE there could be some inconsistency also.

In case of Webgui [Save, Cancel, Put in Process, Postpone, Complete] are displayed in Status Bar.
In case of SE [Save, Cancel] are in Status Bar and [Put in Process, Postpone, Complete] in Toolbar.

Anyway, if Desktop mode of SE has [Save, Cancel] buttons in Status Bar, then these buttons must appear in expanded mode of Mobile Menu Button "...". Compare Original Screen for that case. 



If some button is needed from Toolbar, you can drag it into the Status Bar and it will appear in expanded mode of Mobile Menu Button also.



Further inconsistency of Flavor prepared in Webgui and consumed in SE must be examined and corrected manually.

Best Regards!

entry.title: Re: Error: Fiori tool guided development with OData service v2

entry.title: Unable pass ODN number in reference field in accounting document.
entry.content:
Dear Member ,

we have created condition contract , we have generated settlement document after executing T- code WB2R_SC , credit memo is generated , Now settlement document number is passing as reference number in accounting .

we have to pass OND number in reference number in  accounting document .  

what configuration need to pass ODN number after settlement of condition contract .

Kindly suggest .

Thanks & Regards ,

Shital

entry.title: Re: DTW- Outgoing Payment Upload - Journal Entry
entry.content:
Hi @Thaonguyenphuong ,

Try adding the field InvType to the VPM2 template and use the Journal Entry object in there (30). I think SAP displays this message because it simply doesn't know which object you are referring to.

I hope it helps.

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: Details on Allowed File Types, Sizes, and Number of Attachments in Customer Master Data
entry.content:
Need detailed information regarding the allowed file types, sizes, and the number of attachments that can be uploaded when creating a new customer in the Manage Customer Master Data app.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Details on Allowed File Types, Sizes, and Number of A...
entry.content:
Attachment types:

  • Files: Any type of files with or without the document type. 
  • Links: Stores links to external content to which you can navigate using a uniform resource locator (URL) 

Refer Below documentation for more details:

Manage Customer Master Data-Attachments

File Size :

The Maximum file size allowed is 100MB

Number of Attachments:

There is no restriction on number of attachments

entry.title: SAP MDK: How to R/W values from/to editable DataTable cells?
entry.content:
Hello!

How do I read values in a Rule from an editable DataTableColumnItem ("EditType": "List") in a DataTable OnValueChange and write them to another cell on the same row? The Table is populated via an OData-Service-Binding and the options for the column in question are either an "Object Collection" or an "Object Binding on EntitySet".

The Mentor-App has such an example but I can't find the source code for that.

@bill_froelich @Jitendra_Kansal

entry.title: SAPUI5 app index.html as a landing page on SAPGUI home screen
entry.content:
Hi All,

We're have a requirement to display simple sapui5 app (static html page with news) on initial Easy Access system screen.
We updated SESS_URL field in SSM_CUST table with https://<system_host>:<system_port>/sap/bc/ui5_ui5/sap/<app_bsp_name>/index.html
But we're getting authentication prompt on Easy Access screen, we want to avoid it as we are calling this html page only inside system.
Could you please suggest how we can disable authentication for specific SICF service node?

entry.title: Input control unexpected behavior SAC
entry.content:
I have an issue with input controls in a story for a planning model in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) (the model has no data yet). Despite following best practices, I am experiencing unexpected behavior with my input controls.

I have created an optimized story for a planning model. The story includes input controls - for example selecting currency (EUR, USD, GBP) and payment method (B2B, B2C, P2P).

The issue arises when I select a currency and then choose a payment method; the selection for the currency input control disappears, and all options become checked.

Steps Already Taken:
Input Control Properties: I have verified that the "Enable Cascading" option is turned off for both input controls.
Property Filter Settings: The "Linked Analysis" option is set appropriately to avoid unintended filtering.
Model Settings: The dimensions for currency and payment method in the planning model are not linked or dependent on each other.
Version: I am using the latest version of SAP Analytics Cloud available to me.

I would appreciate assistance in resolving this issue. Could you please provide guidance or further troubleshooting steps to ensure that each input control operates independently?

Thank you

The only thing I find is this one: "Grouping can only be activated for dimension columns."

What ist a "dimension column" ? Can't find any information about this.

Best regards, Matthias

entry.title: Re: Designing UI5 Apps as Business Solution for SAP Build Work Zone - Part 2

It worked without CDM. The issue was with Build Workzone instead, we opened a SAP ticket and two days after this the workzone started to work again. So, the final solution we used was whitout CDM, but just because it was already done, next time I will try to use CDM.

Just to present an overview of the final solution, we've deployed the library as a new business solution in BTP, and in each application we pointed a new destination to the library:

App 1 consuming the library:

App 2 consuming the library:

The xs-app.json

The MTA file (destinations block and resources block):

Regards

entry.title: Re: How to fix OOMKilled restart issue with SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content:
Very interesting @YannickRobin ! 👏 Thanks for the write-up 🙂

Quick question (and forgive me if that's stupid as I've been out of the Commerce loop and the CCv2 stack specifics for some time): As the issue seems to be caused by the default arena sizing by glibc's allocator, as you point out in the article, have others allocators been considered ?

I know other cloud providers favor other allocators over glibc's. A few eons ago, tcmalloc was often mentioned as an alternative, or more recently mimalloc.

Maybe not something anyone can meddle with on CCv2 deployments, but maybe a point to carefully audit with the infrastructure team ?

entry.title: Basics of Automation Orchestration in SAP DM: Web Servers, Services, and Production Processes
entry.content:
SAP Digital Manufacturing (DM) increases industrial productivity by converging business capabilities (IT) with connectivity and automation capabilities (OT):

  • Top-Floor-to-Shop-Floor Connectivity: Using the Production Connectivity Model, you can connect various systems, assets, and web servers for seamless data and service exchange from top floor business applications to shop floor machine operations.
  • End-to-End Automation: Using the Production Process Designer, you can orchestrate services from various web servers to model production processes, then deploy them to automate manufacturing operations from raw material to finished goods, including the automation of physical assets, such as complete robot cells, machineries, conveyor belts, and other equipment.

A production process is a chain of actions that aims to complete a business purpose through a series of service calls. You can create a production process with service calls across the landscape of systems on the top floor and shop floor. The following graphic shows production processes for automating engraving activities.

Note: With production processes, you can also control the flow of operations based on your business logic using control elements such as Script Task, Condition, and Error Catch. For more information, please refer to the official Help Portal Production Process Designer documentation.

Figure 1: Routings, cloud-based production processes, and shop-floor-based automation sequences.

Connectivity is the foundation of automation. To automate processes across your cloud-based systems, on-premise systems, and shop floor systems, you must connect the systems with each other so that they can call each other’s services. In Setting up Shop Floor Connectivity, we’ve explained how to establish a secure connection between a shop floor system and SAP DM using the Production Connector and the Cloud Connector, and how to connect a device on the shop floor using the asset model to read its tags.

Note: There are built-in services for both reading and writing indicators. For more information, refer to Available Services and Subprocesses.

After connecting systems and assets, you can create and connect various web server objects, register the services provided by them, and use the services across your system landscape to create production processes.

In this blog, we’ll explain the conceptual basics of services, web servers, and production processes, the building blocks of automation orchestration.

 

1 - Service Registry

The service registry hosts all necessary (meta-)information on services across the customer’s system landscape, including shop floor, top floor, on-premise, and in the cloud. This information is essential for calling a service and includes details such as its endpoint, input and output payloads, and failure codes. To make a service available for orchestration in the Production Process Designer, you must register it in the service registry by providing its (meta-)information.

Figure 2: A screenshot of the Manage Service Registry app showing a registered service’s meta-information.

 

2 - Web Servers

Web servers are systems that offer REST-based and ODATA-based services.

 

Setting Up Web Servers

In the Manage Web Servers app, you can create digital twins of your physical web servers by providing their (meta-)information and the services they host. The app distinguishes different types of web server objects.

You can manually create web server objects of types “Cloud services” and “On Premise services”:

  • Cloud services:
    This type of web server object represents cloud-based web servers. They offer REST-based services in the cloud (e.g., SAP S/4HANA Cloud).
  • On Premise services:
    This type of web server object represents on-premise-based web servers. They offer REST-based services in the local on-premise network (e.g., an on-premise EWM system, a LIMS system, or a local AGV control offering its functionality via REST)

Some web servers can also host a process runtime, a component that enables the execution of production processes. Process runtimes are offered on DM in the cloud and on the edge, known as the “Process Engine”. They are also offered on Production Connectors, which can be edge-based or standalone MS Windows-based.

These process runtime web servers (hereafter referred to as runtime servers) are represented by web server objects of types “SAP Digital manufacturing services”, “SAP Digital Manufacturing for edge computing services”, “Production Connector services”, and “PCo services” (legacy). They are automatically created when you onboard these systems.

Figure 3: A screenshot of the Manage Web Servers app showing different types of web server objects.

 

Assigning Services to Web Server Objects

You can assign services to web server objects in the Manage Service Registry app. You can also deactivate services in a web server object in the Manage Web Servers app.

Figure 4: An example of available web servers and services. You can assign services to the web servers that provide them.

 

Connecting Runtime Servers with Web Servers

A runtime server can call services offered by itself, as well as services offered by web servers directly connected to this runtime server. To call services from a runtime on a remote dedicated web server, you must connect this runtime server object to the web server object in the Manage Web Servers app.

Figure 5: An example network of connected web server objects. The web servers that host process runtimes are DM, Production Connector A, and Production Connector B. In this scenario, DM can call services offered by itself, Production Connector A and B, and the 3rd party web server. Production Connector B can call services offered by DM, ME, and the 3rd party web server.

Note: When the runtime server is a Production Connector, you can explicitly select which services you want to call from it and then deploy them to the Production Connector. For more information, refer to Adding and Deploying Services from a Connected Web Server.

Note: As service providers, web server objects are counterparts of OPC server objects in the Configure Production Connectivity app. OPC servers use OPC discovery to detect available services. For more information, refer to Configuring Services in Service Provider.

 

3 - Production Processes Designer

You can use the Production Process Designer to create production processes and deploy them to their designated runtimes.

Note: A process deployed to the Process Engine on Cloud or Edge is typically called a production process, while a process deployed to the Production Connector is referred to as an automation sequence.

Creating and Deploying Production Processes

To create a production process, you first select the runtime environment where the production process will be executed. You can only use services offered by the runtime server itself, or services offered by web servers directly connected to this runtime server. For more information, refer to Available Services and Subprocesses.

The available services are shown in the Services and Processes toolbox section of the Design Production Processes app. Services with web server assigned are grouped in root nodes while services without web server assigned are put under one independent folder.

Figure 6: A screenshot of the design interface of the Design Production Processes app.

Figure 7: In this scenario, we create three production processes “PP” for the DM runtime, “Sub PP 1” for the Production Connector A runtime, and “Sub PP 2” for the Production Connector B runtime. Because “PP” is to be deployed to DM runtime, we can only use services offered by DM itself, Production Connector A and B, and the 3rd party web server to create it.

Figure 8: After creating the production processes, we deploy them to their designated runtimes for execution.

 

Next Steps

In the following blogs in our series, we’ll showcase how to create and call production processes in different ways to automate manufacturing processes.

For more information, refer to the user assistance for SAP Digital Manufacturing on the SAP Help Portal.

Do you like this blog? Feel free to tell us what you think in the comments. We appreciate your feedback!

Or, if you have any questions, please check the SAP Community Q&A Area, or comment down below.

entry.title: Re: How to make an infinity scrolling in macros table?
entry.content: Hi, endless scrolling is the default behavior for the macro table. Just checked this in our system (1.108) Does this only happen when including it in the dialog? What version are you on?

entry.title: Re: S4 Public master data migration logic
entry.content:
Hello, this question should be properly asked in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Partner Delivery Group at this link in order to get the quickest answer and other links to previously answered master data questions: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/ct-p/s4hana-cloud-public

 Some partial answers 

1) functional and development test - can be done in starter tenant with limited data. No, Starter system is only for Fit to Standard and will contain all pre-delivered best practice config and org master data. The partner will do only very minor config (if any) and will typically leverage the  pre-delivered Org structure, standard master data and  standard test scripts to guide the walkthroughs.

2) test with real life data - can be done in test tenant with limited data: Yes, in a normal 3 system landscape testing and any data migration test loads are done in T.

3) full migration test with real life data : We traditionally have not seen or supported full mock master data load for a simulated production cutover dry run. Partners and customers have found workarounds using alpha numeric labels to indicate which data load cycle a master data entity belonged to.  

My colleague has a great post around the upcoming SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, three-system landscape, parallel line. Have a look at the section concerning Data Migration, it discusses how we are solving for that scenario

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-three-system-landscape-parallel-line-high/ba-p/13980946

entry.title: Handling Import Tariffs on Steel Products in SAP (USA – March 2024)
entry.content:
The U.S. government has announced that new import tariffs on steel products will take effect starting March 12, 2024. Some of the products exported by our company may be affected, and we need to understand how to handle these tariffs within SAP. We're shipping from Germany to a customer in the US. Occasionally, we also ship to one of our own plants in the US. We're not using GTS.

We are looking for best practices or relevant SAP functionalities to manage import duties effectively. Specifically, we would like to know:

  • How to configure import tariffs/customs duties in SAP (e.g., in SAP SD, MM)?
  • Whether there are OSS Notes or SAP recommendations regarding this topic?
  • How similar cases have been handled in S/4HANA?

If anyone has experience dealing with U.S. import duties in SAP, your insights would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Error when trying to push content on OCN
entry.content:
Hello the community,

We have some issues when pushing content to SAP - OData AP using this endpoint POST /learning/odatav4/public/admin/ocn/v1/OcnCourses.

We are receiving this error:

{ "error": { "code": null, "message": "Error adding OCN course - xxxxxxx" } }

  • Where/how can we find more details about the require that we made?

  • Why the request does not show up in API Center - OData API Audit Log?

Thanks,

entry.title: SAML2 SSO – Extension for Dynamic Attribute Mapping in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We have implemented SAML2 SSO for an SAP S/4HANA On-Premise system using an external Identity Provider (IdP). The authentication process is working as expected, with Name ID Format set to PERSISTENT and user mapping configured in the standard table SAML2_PIDFED.

Challenge

Due to compliance requirements, storing the IdP user ID in SAP for mapping purposes is not permitted. As a result, we are exploring an alternative approach to achieve user mapping without storing the IdP user ID directly.

Proposed Approach

The idea is to extend the SAML2.0 authentication process to call a third-party web service that provides an additional attribute (which can be legally stored) to be used for mapping instead of the IdP user ID. The high-level process would be:

  1. User accesses the SAP Fiori URL, triggering a redirect to the IdP authentication page.
  2. The user logs in via the IdP authentication process.
  3. The IdP sends a SAML2 response containing the IdP user ID.
  4. (Code Extension) SAP calls a third-party web service using the IdP user ID to retrieve a new attribute.
  5. SAP performs user mapping using the new attribute instead of the IdP user ID.
  6. The user is successfully logged into Fiori with their SAP username.

Questions for the Community

  1. Is it possible to extend the SAML2.0 authentication process in SAP S/4HANA to support this approach?
  2. What would be the best extension points (e.g., BAdIs, enhancement spots, user exits) to achieve this customization?
  3. Are there any potential risks or best practices we should consider when modifying the SAML2 authentication flow in this way?

Any guidance, experiences, or references to similar implementations would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you for your insights!

entry.title: Re: Importing Price Lists and Volume Pricing
entry.content: Check with your administrators, they can either create an S-ID for you or download the note as a pdf. (This is SAP copyright material)

entry.title: Handle translations on custom codelist
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

In my SDK solution, codelists have been implemented a few versions ago, and now I need to add french translations in a new patch.

I know that there are some difficulties with the codelist's traductions via the SDK and I've tried to follow this note: https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2926215

Problem is that my imports are never taken in account:

It's never detected, whether I make dummy changes or not.

Is there another way to force the translations ?

 

Thank you for your help,

Marion

entry.title: Re: Managing Multiple Material Numbers in SAP S/4HANA Clo...
entry.content: If I want to create several MPN's for the same vendor, what should I do? In material master data I´m allowed to specify just one MPN. Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: REFX Infinite Building (perpetual term): contract master data & postings (public cloud)
entry.content: Hi Anja & Henrike, thank you for looking into my question! The calculation is rather straightforward, eg with a quarterly payment: =-PV(quarterly discount rate; 4 quarters * 999 years;quarterly payment;;1). In the case we would use 50 years, it would follow the 'finite lease' calculations and saw some differences with infinite leases.

entry.title: Re: Add a custom Text Element to ME59N prog.
entry.content: Any help pls.

entry.title: Re: DTW- Outgoing Payment Upload - Journal Entry
entry.content: The upload was successful. I really appreciate your support!

entry.title: Re: FioriElements - Value-Help for SearchField - data sho...
entry.content:
Hi junwu, thanks again for looking into it...

I was checking documentation again and finally got the problem:
I annotated the @Consumption.ValueHelpDefinition in CDS
and the Common.ValueList in XML...

After removing the CDS-annotation, it works...

Oh my God.... Matthias

entry.title: User is trying to post unplanned dep should post with TTYZ88 & Z89 for only 50 and 51 area
entry.content:
Hello All,
A user is attempting to post an unplanned depreciation but it should only be allowed with TTY Z88 and Z89 for areas 50 and 51. It should not be allowed for other depreciation areas.

Can someone assist with resolving this issue?

Thanks in Advance

entry.title: Unlocking Continuous Quality Checks (CQCs) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Are you an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customer and interested in requesting and learning more about how the available CQC services can support your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition solution? If so, then I invite you list to our below E-learning.

  • Unlocking Continuous Quality Checks (CQCs) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

(Before you can access the link, a one-time registration in the SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required. A detailed step-by-step guide on how to register can be found here.)

Service Overview:

ServiceAimWhen to UseAdditional Information
CQC for Transformation AssessmentGauge/provide feedback on your project team’s awareness of topics and resources that are critical to project success

Prepare session in prepare phase

Realize session in the realize phase

  • Information sheet
  • Roadmap Viewer
CQC for Deployment ReadinessPre go-live SAP best practice review of key SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition settings

Two months prior to go live

  • Information sheet
  • Roadmap Viewer
CQC for Process Adoption

Service will review the usage of your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition solution and help you improve current business processes and adopt new business processes.

Run phase at least 6 months after go-live
  • Information sheet
  • Roadmap Viewer
CQC for Data Volume Management

Review the solution under data volume management and information lifecycle management aspects

Run phase at least 6 months after go-liveIn development – if interested please reach out to your SAP Contact

All CQCs listed above are included as part of your SAP Enterprise Support cloud edition support maintenance contract which is embedded in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition subscription with no additional costs. Please note you can also find a complied list of our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition learnings here - SAP Enterprise Support Learning Assets for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Meet-the-Expert Sessions and E-Learnings.

In addition, please sign up for the value map for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to receive full guidance on CQCs and get personalized guidance on your cloud ERP journey. In the value map, you can collaborate in forums, ask questions, or use the “Request a Call” feature to speak with an expert. To learn more about value maps, watch the following video.

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK: How to R/W values from/to editable DataTable...
entry.content:
Can you please provide more details on where / what you see in the Mentor App?

You can access the changes using the getChanges API method.  You may want to review the What's New blog when we introduced the feature in MDK 23.3

entry.title: Can we create BP without Vendor/Customer role in S/4 using LTMC?
entry.content: Can we create BP without Vendor role or Customer role using any LTMC templates? Need to load BP without customer role but need to load Sales data and Company code data. Is it technically possible to load Sales and Company data using Customer Master LTMC template?

entry.title: Re: Background Data is updated in Candidate Profile but n...
entry.content:
Hi @Gi ,

PFB note from implementation guide:

Problem Stmt: There are few fields on candidate profile which are not visible to Quick Apply applicant. And at later stage the candidate is entering the data on candidate profile but same is not helping to reflect it over job application.

 

Proposed Solution:
1) Create a new dedicated JRDM and Job Application template for Quick Apply

2) Include the affected fields in newly created Job Application Template

3) Hide the newly included fields from step 2 using Multi-stage Application

4) Identify the point/status from the talent pipeline to ask for input from candidate against fields included in step 2

5) Make the fields visible to Candidate and Recruiter at this stage

 

I hope this approach will be helpful for you.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Errors on revision date in curriculum connector
entry.content: tried with one sample directly into notepad but it still adding a 0

entry.title: Are you familiar with single level cost estimates?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Introducing Product Cost Planning and Production Accounting in SAP S/HANA learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey will guide business users through understanding and updating the standard price of a product, while familiarizing themselves with single level cost estimates. This learning journeys addresses learners who are interested in SAP S/4HANA Cloud and on premise deployment options. 

Learning objectives
Upon completing this learning journey, business users will be able to understand and apply various cost estimation techniques, including evaluating item categories and technical quantity structures, utilizing master data, and accurately valuating and releasing standard prices within a business environment.

Prerequisites

  • Outlining Cost Management and Profitability Analysis in SAP S/4HANA
  • Performing Overhead Cost Controlling​ in SAP S/4HANA

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Managing Multiple Material Numbers in SAP S/4HANA Clo...
entry.content: Perform some search on EINA-IDNLF 'Material Number Used by Vendor'

entry.title: Re: Create Variable for 0HIER_VERS
entry.content: It's been a while but i think the solution was to create the variable in Eclipse

entry.title: Re: Can you remember the first time you heard of SAP
entry.content: Hi Ruthvik, I first heard of SAP while working at Storage Technology Corporation, in the mid 90s!  I was working at Storage as a Field Support Analyst for Service Billing on the Tape Library's we sold, and they decided to replace their home grown legacy systems with SAP 3.1H.  Turned out SAP released Service Management with 3.1 H and I was invited to join the program and learn how to implement Order to Cash and Service Management!  It was the beginning of my long association with SAP.  I worked for the mother ship from 2003 until 2017!  I now help SAP customers, locally, as an SAP Testing Project Manager!

entry.title: Skip bin section RF scan from storage bin field in EWM
entry.content:
Hi gurus,

There is the possibility to skip the bin section (storage behavior: Pallet) counter from the storage bin scan in RF?

In the image we see that the main bin is the 110201 and the subbin is the 110201/1, where the bin section is represented with "/1", this counter can be avoided and only scan the main bin (110201) directly from RF scan?

Mainly because the customer doesn't want to relabel the storage bins from the warehouse.

Thanks

Best regards

entry.title: Re: User is trying to post unplanned dep should post with...
entry.content: Check your Customizing : Asset Accounting, Depreciation, Unplanned Depreciation, Define Transaction Types for Unplanned Depreciation, Define /and/ Limit to Depreciation Areas

entry.title: Re: Working with RAP BO Events end-to-end - Event-driven-architecture - Event framework
entry.content:
Local Events is one of the most powerful tools I've seen in SAP in a long time. More over if we combine it with BALI Logs to track the behavior of the code triggered by the event.

It's a shame that SAP doesn't have an app to display the logs in Cloud Public, just like we have done during decades with tcode SLG1: https://community.sap.com/t5/general-and-cross-topics-q-a/is-it-possible-to-display-custom-application-logs-in-s-4hana-cloud-public/qaq-p/14008266

entry.title: Re: Stay certified - Is an SAP Learning Hub subscription...
entry.content:
Hi @mb1011 (Mihael)
Pease follow these  steps to stay certified

1. Get an SAP Universal ID
Make sure you have a valid SAP Universal ID with at least two email accounts assigned to it. If you don’t, watch the video for instructions.
2. Ensure you have an SAP Learning Hub subscription to access the assessment to stay certified
If you have no subscription, you’ll need to book and retake the full 2-3 hour SAP Certification exam every year. Check your subscription or subscribe to SAP Learning Hub.
3. Check your certification status
Log in to My Learning to see your certifications and their expiration dates.
Make sure you complete the short annual assessment as soon as possible to extend the validity of your certification by 12 months. 
If the certification has been retired and you want to retain your certified status, you need to re-certify in an alternative certification. Find recommended alternatives using the SAP Certification finder

4. Prepare for the assessment
Find preparation courses or live sessions linked under your certification.

5. Take the assessment
The assessment is a short, non-proctored quiz with unlimited attempts. Once you’ve completed it successfully, the validity of your certification will be extended by 12 months.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Get Discovered as a Supplier on SAP Business Network
entry.content:
With over $69 billion in business opportunities posted on the network last year, there is no shortage of potential for suppliers to get discovered by global buyers and grow their business. You can get started building your presence on SAP Business Network today, whether you have zero or many customers on there today. 

Optimizing your profile for better leads  

As buyers search for products and services on SAP Business Network, information from supplier company profiles, in addition to network catalog content, is used to populate the search results. By having a complete, and up to date profile, you can optimize your chances of generating leads.  

In particular, the following criteria from company profiles is used in buyer searches:  

  • Company name- Identify who you are to buyers  
  • Certificates- Build trust and credibility by demonstrating your commitment to quality standards, expertise in your field, adherence to industry best practices, etc.  
  • Keywords- Increase searchability when a buyer search matches your keywords  
  • Ship to/service locations- Show buyers if they are in your service range  
  • Product and service categories- Help potential customers understand your offerings and increase your searchability  

Check out this helpful checklist to make sure you fully complete your profile and increase your chances of receiving leads from interested buyers.  

Generating leads with a network catalog  

A network catalog is a single master catalog that can be created via file upload. It is searchable by keyword and viewable by all buyers on SAP Business Network. Companies can include both products and services, regardless of how they are measured, priced, or delivered, in their catalog. Each catalog item includes fields for a name, description, photo, specs, price, and a link to the uploading supplier’s company profile.  

What are the benefits?  

  • Uploading a network catalog provides increased visibility for the company and their offerings, as well as high quality lead generation.   
  • By uploading a searchable network catalog, suppliers can share their entire catalog with all buyers on SAP Business Network.   
  • This allows suppliers to be able to upload and maintain all content in one source.  
  • These buyers are ready to buy the products and services offered by the supplier as they are searching for specific content on the network.   
  • Once a buyer finds a product or service they are interested in, they can send a request for information (RFI) via the price check button on the product listing, which will show up in the supplier’s leads dashboard within the portal.  

Maximize your potential for leads  

Launching in February 2025, the SAP Business Network, promote subscription will offer tools designed to fully maximize your potential for leads as a network supplier, such as unlimited network catalog publishing edits, open APIs, GenAI capabilities, profile verification, insights and more. It will be available for purchase in the portal. Visit this page to learn more about promote.  

Complete Your Profile and Upload Your Catalog Today  

If you don’t already have an account, you can register for a free SAP Business Network, standard account in just a few minutes, or log in if you already have an account to get started. All standard and enterprise accounts will be able to upload a network via file upload with a limit of 1000 publishing edits. For unlimited publishing capabilities and additional catalog tools, suppliers can purchase the promote subscription directly in the portal. 

Register or login to complete your profile and catalog today.

entry.title: Message no. FF707 SAP, is a warning message
entry.content:
The message no. FF707 SAP, is a warning message but when the amount is 0, it writes two rows in the BSET table:
1. one with zero vat amount
1. one with the correct value

does anyone know how to solve? i.e. not to have the double row written in the BSET?

entry.title: Re: Which course should i take to clear the fiori develop...
entry.content:
Hi @dasarijit 

Please access our C_FIORD SAP Certified Associate - SAP Fiori Application Developer certification site.
There you can find the How to prepare section and sample question for self-evaluation.

Please access the C_FIOAD SAP Certified Associate - SAP Fiori System Administration  certification site.
there you can find the How to prepare  and sample question for self-evaluation.

All the best
Margit

entry.title: Re: Assign multiple valuation methodes in FCV to one Vali...
entry.content:
Hello Felix,

Within a rule it is possible to define more than one valuation rule step.

Example:

You have a rule called ABCD.

Within the rule you define a valuation rule step 10 and a valuation rule step 20.

To the step 10 you can assign the FX semantic tag and the method always valuate.

To the rule step 20, you can assign the FX2 semantic tag and the method lower cost or market principle, as reuquired.

I hope this answers you question.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Message no. FF707 SAP, is a warning message
entry.content:
Did you considert switching back the message to an error ?

  • Reverse of 3422955 - Error FF707 for FI Posting transactions.
  • Other solutions in 3533781 - FF707 for FB01 or BAPI / Customizing or BAdI 2449228 - Changing message FF707 using BAdI

entry.title: Re: Availability check on MO with open OBD
entry.content: Can you elaborate your question in the above description? What abnormality did you find? Can you share screenshot if possible?

entry.title: IAS Custom Schema with Multivalue Field Propagation to SAP CAP
entry.content:
Hello experts!

We are using a Custom Schema from SAP Cloud Identity Service to indicate user data, such as the centers the user has access to, to restrict data access for a CAP application.

We are propagating this in the Cloud Identity Service application to be consumed both in a UI5 application, via a request to the sap-approuter-userapi service, and to restrict data access in CAP with the @restrict annotation.

In Cloud Identity Service, we parameterize users as follows:

The problem is that, instead of seeing an array of strings in the API, we are seeing a string with the array value inside.

In the request to the sap-approuter-userapi service, at the attributes endpoint, we get:

When we expected to get an array of strings as in the scopes or Groups properties.

At CAP filters level, we have:

And debugging the filters, the value that arrives in the read implementation would be the following:

This causes the restrict not to work as it filters by the string and not by the N values we parameterized in SAP Cloud Identity Service.

In the attribute type configuration in xs-security.json, we have defined it as “s” because we have not found in the documentation how to define it as an object or an array of strings.

We have found a workaround both in UI5 and in the CAP implementations of the “on” READ to transform that string into an array of strings and create the restrict filters object, but we believe the behavior should be that both the sap-approuter-userapi service API and the automatic implementation in CAP of the user parameters should return an array of strings.

Is this the standard behaviour of custom schema attributes marked as multivalue or are we missing some configuration?

Thank you in advance for your help,

Alejandro.

entry.title: Re: MTD, YTD, YTG (YearToGo) Calculations in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Actually, the question was referring only to the SAP Could Excel Add-in. 
I am familiar with YTD calculations in SAP Cloud.

entry.title: PM: Why aren't additional resources visible on the screen when I add a new one to the team section?
entry.content:
Hello experts,

While creating a customer project in Project Management, I attempted to add a new additional resource under the Team section, but the additional resource area did not appear on the screen. I checked the IMG settings and product master data and they were completed. Additionally, the expense area has also disappeared.

I am not sure what else to check to resolve this issue. Please advise. Thank you for your help,

Wendy

entry.title: Re: Reimagining digital learning as an integrated part of a Digital Adoption Platform with SAP &&&&
entry.content:
@lben 

The general availability of the new WalkMe offering has not yet been announced, so it cannot be delayed. 

A new SAP Enable Now edition focused on digital learning is planned as an interim solution for new customers until the new WalkMe offering is available.

entry.title: Re: fill Component list from maintenance plans work orders
entry.content:
Hello @JoshCotton ,

You can do it in task list operation screen where you will find the component button. 

Once you click the component button, you will navigate to the component tab where you can assign the spare parts.

 

Save it. Once you assign the task list to the maintenance plan and trigger the order, the components (spare parts) will be copied to maintenance order.

Vaibhav

entry.title: Re: Value Accelerator Event - Structure question
entry.content: Hello Fabian thank you for your answer. Which kind of confirm what I understood. What I am not clear of though : is why do we need a timestamp. Indeed if my invoice is due today it will be overdue tomorrow (D+1) : no need to set a 23:59:59 time stamp for that ! Have a nice day. Sanddie

entry.title: Re: Input control unexpected behavior SAC
entry.content:
Hi @Deb_D,

As you mentioned earlier you have no data in model 

so make sure that in input control (Filter) setting select "Unbooked members" On 

Kindly upvote if you find it helpful.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP ASE error log message : Use lastsave as start poi...
entry.content: Thanks !!

entry.title: Re: Call an OData Web API Service externally
entry.content: are you sure there is no "-web" in the host part?

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK: How to R/W values from/to editable DataTable...
entry.content:
Thank you for answering so quickly.
Unfortunately, we are not able to retrieve changes using

 

clientAPI.getControl('SectionedTable0').getSection('SectionDataTable0').getChanges();

 

depending on whether we use getPageProxy() or not we either get an empty array or an empty object.
And the Mentor app example I was referring to is the Mentor demo app o

clientAPI.getControl('SectionedTable0').getSection('SectionDataTable0').getChanges();

--or--

clientAPI.getPageProxy().getControl('SectionedTable0').getSection('SectionDataTable0').getChanges();

f the client app. In there, the data table showcase has our desired functionality.

entry.title: Re: From User Exits to RAP: Adopting a Clean Core Approach in S/4HANA
entry.content: Thank you for the detailed explanation @RavdeepSingh ....
